name: OLMo 3-Think 32B
archDisplay: Dense transformer (decoder-only, sliding/full attention mix, exposed CoT)
note: Latest flagship and strongest fully open reasoning model; narrows the gap to Qwen 3-32B-Thinking on reasoning benchmarks while trained on ~6x fewer tokens.

results.notes: GPQA Diamond, measured by Artificial Analysis (Intelligence Index v4.0), republished on the fetched page; NOTE: Ai2's own reported GPQA 58.1 (HF card/blog/paper) uses the gpqa_main split per allenai/olmes task config, not Diamond, so the independent Diamond number is used here
Humanity's Last Exam, no tools; Artificial Analysis measurement (Intelligence Index v4.0), republished on the fetched page; not reported by Ai2 in the OLMo 3 blog/card/paper

MMLU-Pro; Artificial Analysis measurement (Intelligence Index v4.0), republished on the fetched page; Ai2 reports MMLU-Pro only for OLMo 3 base models (OlmoBaseEval held-out), not the Think 32B post-trained model
Currently missing from this model's results despite being on the primary card; Qwen 3 32B scores 88.8, Qwen 3 VL 32B Thinking 90.1
AGI Eval English subset; not previously tracked for this model. Same column-verified HF README table as the AIME 2025 addition.

verificationNotes: Existence confirmed via allenai.org/blog/olmo3 and press coverage (Nov 20, 2025). paramsB re-derived from model.safetensors.index.json total ≈32.2B (matches 32.23 within rounding). config.json confirms max_position_embeddings=65536 (~64K) and hybrid sliding/full attention across 64 layers, and no tool-use/function-calling exposed on this model card (matches capability flags). All 6 result rows independently confirmed on the HF model card table, including every cited Qwen 3 32B comparison number (MATH 95.4, AIME 80.8, BBH 90.6, HumanEvalPlus 91.2, IFEval 86.5, LiveCodeBench 90.2) except the 'Qwen 3 VL 32B Thinking 96.7' aside which I could not independently re-confirm in my fetch (left in notes as unverified color, does not affect the primary score).
